channel.title: Surfaced — Discover What's Next
channel.description: Fresh discoveries, trending products, hidden gems, future tech, and daily tools — curated every day.

item.title: DaVinci Resolve
item.description: DaVinci Resolve is a professional, all-in-one video editing, color correction, visual effects, and audio post-production software. It offers an incredibly powerful free version that many professionals use, with an optional paid Studio version for advanced features. The software's industry-leading color grading tools are particularly renowned, allowing for precise and cinematic color manipulation that is difficult to achieve elsewhere.

item.title: Retool
item.description: Retool is a low-code platform that allows you to build custom internal tools and applications quickly. You can drag and drop pre-built components like tables, forms, and charts, and connect them to your databases and APIs with minimal coding. Retool offers a free tier for individuals and small teams, with paid plans scaling based on usage and features. It's exceptionally good at creating dashboards and data management interfaces that would otherwise require significant development time.

item.title: LibreOffice
item.description: LibreOffice is a comprehensive, free, and open-source office productivity suite that provides powerful alternatives to proprietary software. It includes applications for word processing (Writer), spreadsheets (Calc), presentations (Impress), vector graphics and flowcharts (Draw), database management (Base), and formula editing (Math). LibreOffice is compatible with a wide range of document formats, including Microsoft Office files, ensuring seamless collaboration. It offers a robust set of features suitable for both personal and professional use, without any cost or licensing fees.

item.title: Figma
item.description: Figma is a cloud-based design tool primarily used for user interface (UI) and user experience (UX) design, but it's also versatile enough for wireframing, prototyping, and even graphic design. Its standout feature is real-time collaboration, allowing multiple designers to work on the same file simultaneously, making it ideal for team projects. Figma offers a free tier with generous features for individual designers and small teams, with paid plans providing more advanced capabilities for larger organizations. It simplifies the design process by offering a unified platform for creation, prototyping, and handoff to developers.

item.title: Clera
item.description: Clera is an AI-powered talent agent designed to help individuals find job opportunities that genuinely align with their aspirations. It operates through conversational interfaces like iMessage and WhatsApp, learning your career goals, preferences, and desired work environments. Clera then proactively surfaces relevant job openings, acting as a personalized recruiter to streamline your job search. It's particularly adept at filtering out irrelevant listings and presenting you with roles you'd be truly excited about, a feat often challenging with traditional job boards. Clera offers a freemium model, with core functionalities available at no cost.

item.title: Awesome Self-Hosted
item.description: Awesome Self-Hosted is a meticulously curated list of free and open-source software that can be installed and run on one's own server infrastructure. It covers a vast array of categories, including productivity, media, communication, and security tools, providing links to projects and essential setup information. This resource is invaluable for individuals and organizations looking to gain more control over their data, avoid subscription fees, and customize their digital environments. It helps users discover and evaluate self-hostable alternatives to popular proprietary services, with a strong emphasis on privacy and ownership.

item.title: LLM Course
item.description: LLM Course is a comprehensive, open-source educational resource dedicated to teaching the fundamentals and advanced concepts of Large Language Models (LLMs). It covers topics ranging from the underlying transformer architecture and training methodologies to practical applications and ethical considerations. The course material is freely accessible, providing lectures, code examples, and reading materials for self-paced learning. It stands out by offering a structured and in-depth curriculum designed by experts in the field, making complex LLM topics digestible for students and professionals alike.

item.title: Stable Diffusion Web UI Docker
item.description: Stable Diffusion Web UI Docker is a pre-configured Docker image that simplifies the setup and deployment of the popular Stable Diffusion Web UI, an open-source graphical interface for generating AI art. It packages all necessary dependencies and configurations, allowing users to run a powerful image generation model with minimal technical hassle. This solution is free to use and leverages Docker's containerization technology to ensure a consistent and reproducible environment across different systems. Its strength lies in making the complex process of installing and running Stable Diffusion accessible to a wider audience.

item.title: rebuild-openai-api-wrapper
item.description: This project provides a simplified Python wrapper for interacting with the OpenAI API, making it easier for developers to integrate advanced AI capabilities like natural language processing, text generation, and image creation into their applications. It abstracts away much of the complexity of direct API calls, offering a more intuitive interface for common tasks. The wrapper is open-source and free to use, aiming to lower the barrier to entry for developers wanting to leverage cutting-edge AI models. It excels at providing a clean and efficient way to handle API requests and responses for common generative AI use cases.

item.title: nature-skills
item.description: nature-skills is a Python library designed to help researchers and academics create publication-quality figures and visualizations that adhere to the stylistic standards of top scientific journals like Nature. It streamlines the process of generating complex scientific plots, offering pre-built templates and functions for common chart types used in fields such as biology, chemistry, and physics. The library is free to use and open-source, with its primary value lying in its ability to simplify and standardize the visual communication of scientific data, saving researchers significant time and effort in data visualization.

item.title: Streamlaide
item.description: Streamlaide is an AI-powered platform designed to simplify video creation for content creators. It allows users to generate videos from text or existing content, automatically adding visuals, music, and voiceovers. The tool offers a freemium model, providing basic video generation for free with tiered paid plans for more extensive features, higher resolution exports, and longer video lengths. Streamlaide excels at rapidly transforming written content into engaging video formats, significantly reducing the time and technical skill typically required for video production.

item.title: PhotoMath
item.description: PhotoMath is a mobile application that uses your device's camera to instantly solve mathematical problems. Simply point your camera at a math equation – from basic arithmetic to calculus – and PhotoMath will provide the answer, along with step-by-step explanations. The core scanning and solving functionality is free, with an optional premium subscription for more advanced features and ad-free experience. It's unparalleled in its ability to quickly and accurately decipher handwritten or printed math problems and offer clear, guided solutions.

item.title: Affine
item.description: Affine is a versatile productivity application designed to be an all-in-one workspace for individuals and teams. It combines note-taking, project management, and document creation into a single, intuitive interface. Affine offers a free tier with generous features for personal use, with paid plans for advanced collaboration and storage. Its strength lies in its seamless integration of different productivity tools, allowing you to manage tasks, write documents, and organize information without context switching.

item.title: Open-codesign
item.description: Open-codesign is an open-source alternative to proprietary AI design tools, designed to convert prompts into interactive prototypes, slides, and PDFs. It offers a one-click import for your Claude Code or Codex API key, allowing seamless integration with powerful AI models. Supporting a multi-model approach, it works with Claude, GPT, Gemini, Kimi, GLM, and Ollama. The platform emphasizes Bring Your Own Key (BYOK) and local-first functionality for enhanced privacy and control, making it a versatile and secure option for prompt-based design generation.

item.title: Huashu Design
item.description: Huashu Design is a groundbreaking tool that leverages HTML as its native design language for AI code generation. It enables users to create high-fidelity prototypes, presentations, and even animations directly from code. The system incorporates over 20 design philosophies, a 5-dimensional review process, and supports MP4 export, making it an agent-agnostic solution for rapid visual development. It's particularly effective for translating complex ideas into visual formats with remarkable precision.

item.title: Superhuman
item.description: Superhuman is an email client designed for speed and efficiency, aiming to help you achieve Inbox Zero faster than ever. It employs keyboard shortcuts for virtually every action, intelligent sorting, and features like 'undo send' and 'scheduled send'. It's a premium, paid service focused on delivering a premium user experience for professionals who rely heavily on email communication and want to reclaim their time.

item.title: Todo.txt
item.description: Todo.txt is a minimalist, command-line-based to-do list manager that emphasizes simplicity and portability. It allows users to manage tasks using a plain text file, making it accessible from virtually any device or operating system. Tasks can be organized using contexts (e.g., @work) and projects (e.g., +home), with support for due dates, priorities, and recurring tasks. The tool is free and open-source, with a philosophy of keeping the core functionality lightweight and easy to understand.

item.title: lingbot-map
item.description: lingbot-map is a sophisticated feed-forward 3D foundation model designed for reconstructing complex scenes from streaming data. It excels at taking continuous inputs, such as from sensors or video feeds, and building a coherent, three-dimensional representation of the environment in real-time. This Python-based project focuses on efficiency and accuracy, making it suitable for applications in robotics, augmented reality, and autonomous systems where understanding spatial context is paramount. The project is open-source, promoting accessibility and further development.

item.title: browser-harness
item.description: browser-harness is a self-healing harness designed to empower Large Language Models (LLMs) to complete any task by enabling them to interact with web browsers effectively. It provides a robust framework for LLMs to navigate websites, extract information, and perform actions, overcoming common issues like broken selectors or unexpected page changes through its self-healing capabilities. This open-source Python project aims to bridge the gap between LLM intelligence and real-world web interaction. It operates on a free, open-source model, encouraging broad adoption and contribution.

item.title: OpenMythos
item.description: OpenMythos is a theoretical reconstruction of the Claude Mythos architecture, built from first principles using available research literature. It aims to demystify the inner workings of advanced AI models by providing a framework that can be understood and experimented with by developers and researchers. The project is released under an open-source model, fostering community collaboration and advancement in AI understanding. Its primary function is to serve as an educational and experimental tool for understanding complex AI systems.

item.title: Muse
item.description: Muse is a visually oriented note-taking and idea-capture app that uses an infinite canvas. It allows users to combine text, images, drawings, audio, and links in a freeform space, making it ideal for brainstorming, mind mapping, and visual organization. Muse offers a freemium model. It stands out for its intuitive drag-and-drop interface and the ability to freely arrange elements, fostering a more natural and creative approach to capturing and developing ideas than traditional linear note formats.

item.title: Ark (Desktop App)
item.description: Ark is a desktop application that acts as a central hub for managing your digital assets, such as photos, videos, documents, and creative projects. It provides powerful tools for organizing, tagging, searching, and previewing files across different locations on your computer. Ark employs a freemium model, with advanced features available in paid tiers. Its primary value lies in its ability to create a unified, searchable library of all your digital content, regardless of where it's stored, significantly reducing the time spent hunting for files.

item.title: Canny
item.description: Canny is a comprehensive platform designed for collecting, prioritizing, and organizing product feedback. It allows companies to create a public or private feedback portal where users can submit ideas, vote on existing suggestions, and track the status of features. Canny operates on a paid subscription model. Its standout feature is the seamless integration of user feedback into the product development lifecycle, providing a clear roadmap of what users genuinely want.

item.title: codeburn
item.description: codeburn is an interactive Terminal User Interface (TUI) dashboard designed to provide cost observability for AI coding tools like Claude Code, Codex, and Cursor. It helps developers understand exactly where their AI token usage is going, allowing them to monitor and manage expenses associated with AI-powered coding assistance. The tool offers a clear, visual breakdown of token consumption, enabling users to identify areas of potential overspending or inefficient usage. codeburn is a free, open-source project hosted on GitHub, built with TypeScript. It addresses the growing concern of rising AI service costs by bringing transparency to LLM interactions within the development workflow.

item.title: ds4
item.description: ds4 (DeepSeek 4 Flash) is a highly optimized local inference engine designed specifically for Apple Silicon Macs with Metal GPU support. It allows users to run large language models (LLMs) directly on their own hardware, bypassing cloud dependencies and offering enhanced privacy and speed. This open-source tool is written in C, prioritizing efficiency and performance. Its primary value lies in its ability to leverage the unique architecture of Apple Silicon for rapid LLM execution, making advanced AI capabilities accessible without dedicated servers or expensive cloud subscriptions. The tool is freely available on GitHub.

item.title: Bevy
item.description: Bevy is a free, open-source, data-driven game engine built in Rust, designed for rapid development and performance. It leverages the power of the Rust programming language for safety and speed, while its architecture is inspired by data-oriented design principles. Bevy focuses on providing a modular and extensible framework, making it approachable for beginners while offering the depth needed for complex projects. It's an excellent choice for indie game developers, hobbyists, and anyone interested in exploring modern game development paradigms without the cost of proprietary engines. Bevy is particularly adept at creating efficient and scalable game logic through its robust entity-component-system (ECS) architecture.

item.title: Google Docs
item.description: Google Docs is a free, web-based word processor that allows users to create, edit, and collaborate on documents in real-time. It offers a familiar interface with robust formatting tools, templates, and the ability to insert images, tables, and drawings. The key differentiator is its seamless real-time collaboration feature, allowing multiple users to work on the same document simultaneously, with changes visible instantly. Documents are automatically saved to Google Drive, ensuring they are accessible from any device. It’s particularly effective for group projects and co-authoring, making remote teamwork effortless.

item.title: Typefully
item.description: Typefully is a streamlined writing platform specifically built for crafting engaging social media content, particularly for Twitter (X). It provides a distraction-free environment with advanced writing tools like AI-powered suggestions, readability checks, and character count optimization, ensuring your message is clear and impactful. Typefully focuses on helping users write better threads, individual posts, and schedule them efficiently. It operates on a freemium model, offering core writing features for free and paid tiers for advanced analytics, unlimited scheduling, and team collaboration. Its strength lies in its specialized focus on making social media writing effective and efficient.

item.title: Memoria
item.description: Memoria is a private and secure digital journaling application designed to help users capture and reflect on their daily lives. It goes beyond simple text entries by allowing users to enrich their journal with photos, videos, location data, and even mood tracking. The app prioritizes user privacy with end-to-end encryption and local storage options, ensuring personal thoughts remain confidential. Memoria offers a one-time purchase for full functionality, with no subscriptions. It is particularly adept at creating rich, multimedia-filled journal entries that serve as a comprehensive personal archive.

item.title: Vivaldi Browser
item.description: Vivaldi is a highly customizable web browser built for power users, offering unparalleled control over your browsing experience. It goes far beyond basic tab management with features like tab stacking, tiling, and a command chain system for automating tasks. Vivaldi is free to use and runs on Chromium, ensuring compatibility with most websites and extensions. It's particularly adept at managing complex browsing sessions with numerous open tabs, allowing users to organize and switch between them efficiently and without performance degradation, a task many simpler browsers struggle with.

item.title: Evernote
item.description: Evernote is a powerful note-taking and organization app that allows users to capture ideas, to-do lists, photos, voice memos, and web clippings in a single, searchable database. It supports rich text formatting, tagging, and notebook organization to keep information structured and accessible. Evernote operates on a freemium model, offering a robust free tier with limitations on monthly uploads and device syncing, and paid tiers that unlock advanced features and unlimited usage. It excels at rapidly capturing and finding any piece of information you've stored, making it a digital brain for your personal and professional life.

item.title: Martha
item.description: Martha is an AI-powered research and writing assistant designed to help users gather information, synthesize complex topics, and generate initial drafts of written content. It allows you to input a topic, and Martha will scour the web for relevant sources, present them in an organized manner, and help you understand the key arguments and evidence. The tool operates on a freemium model, offering basic research capabilities for free with premium subscriptions unlocking more extensive search parameters, advanced synthesis tools, and longer output limits. Martha's strength lies in its ability to quickly provide a structured overview of a subject, saving immense time in the initial research phase.

item.title: Arc Browser
item.description: Arc Browser is a reimagined web browser built for a more organized and efficient online experience. It features a unique sidebar interface that keeps your tabs, bookmarks, and downloads neatly organized, allowing for quick switching between tasks and projects. Instead of traditional tabs, Arc uses 'spaces' and 'pinnable' tabs to categorize your browsing. It offers a freemium model with core features available at no cost, while advanced functionalities might be part of future paid tiers. Arc's standout feature is its intuitive tab management system, which significantly reduces tab clutter and enhances focus.

item.title: Fireflies.ai
item.description: Fireflies.ai is an AI-powered assistant that automatically records, transcribes, and summarizes your meetings across various platforms like Zoom, Google Meet, and Microsoft Teams. It integrates with your calendar to join scheduled calls and takes detailed notes, identifying action items and key decisions. The tool offers a freemium model, with paid tiers unlocking more advanced features like in-depth analytics and custom integrations. It excels at providing searchable transcripts and concise summaries, saving significant time on post-meeting follow-up.

item.title: Plurai
item.description: Plurai is an AI development tool designed to ensure the reliability and safety of AI agents. It allows users to define specific behaviors and constraints for their AI agents, acting as guardrails. Plurai then generates training data based on these specifications, validates the data to ensure it aligns with the intended guidelines, and deploys a custom-trained model in mere minutes. This streamlined process accelerates the development and deployment of dependable AI agents for various use cases. The service operates on a freemium model, offering basic features for free with paid tiers for advanced capabilities and higher usage limits.

item.title: Slack
item.description: Slack is a widely used business communication platform that replaces email for internal team communication. It organizes conversations into channels based on projects, teams, or topics, allowing for more focused discussions and easier knowledge sharing. Slack supports direct messaging, voice and video calls, and integrates with a vast array of third-party applications, creating a centralized hub for workflow. It offers a free tier with limitations on message history and integrations, alongside paid plans for businesses requiring more robust features and support.

item.title: Google Keep
item.description: Google Keep is a free, simple, and versatile note-taking application that allows users to quickly capture ideas, create to-do lists, record voice memos, and add images. Notes are organized using color-coding and labels, and they sync automatically across all devices signed into a Google account. It's designed for rapid capture and easy retrieval of information, making it ideal for everyday reminders and spontaneous thoughts. Google Keep's strength lies in its intuitive interface and seamless integration with the Google ecosystem.

item.title: Grammarly
item.description: Grammarly is an AI-powered writing assistant that helps users improve their writing by checking for grammar, spelling, punctuation, clarity, engagement, and delivery errors. It provides real-time suggestions and explanations to help users learn and become better writers. Grammarly offers a free version with essential checks and a premium subscription for advanced features like tone detection, style suggestions, and plagiarism detection. It integrates seamlessly with various platforms, including web browsers, email clients, and document editors.

item.title: design.md
item.description: DESIGN.md is a novel format specification designed to bridge the gap between visual design and code generation, particularly for AI agents. It provides a structured, persistent, and machine-readable way to define a visual identity and design system. This allows coding agents to understand and consistently apply brand guidelines, UI elements, and design principles to their output. The format aims to create a shared language for designers and AI developers, ensuring that AI-generated code accurately reflects intended visual aesthetics. It's currently an open specification with significant interest from the developer community.

item.title: Readwise
item.description: Readwise is a popular service that consolidates your highlights and notes from various reading platforms like Kindle, Instapaper, Pocket, and even PDFs into a single, searchable database. It then resurfaces these highlights daily via email or its own app, helping you retain what you've learned. Readwise operates on a freemium model, offering a limited free trial before requiring a paid subscription.

item.title: React Developer Tools
item.description: React Developer Tools is a browser extension for Chrome and Firefox that allows you to inspect and debug React component hierarchies, props, and state. It enables developers to efficiently identify performance bottlenecks, understand component relationships, and trace the flow of data within their React applications. This tool is an essential part of the React development workflow and is completely free to use.

item.title: copy-fail-CVE-2026-31431
item.description: copy-fail-CVE-2026-31431 is a demonstration and proof-of-concept project detailing a critical Local Privilege Escalation (LPE) vulnerability discovered in the Linux kernel. This vulnerability, identified by Theori's Xint Code, exploits a flaw that has existed for approximately nine years. The project provides the necessary code and explanations for security researchers and system administrators to understand, reproduce, and potentially mitigate this significant security risk. It is hosted on GitHub and written in Python.

item.title: mhr-cfw
item.description: mhr-cfw is a Python-based Domain-Fronting Relay designed to bypass network censorship and surveillance by routing traffic through seemingly legitimate services. It acts as a relay, forwarding your internet requests through Google Apps Script (GAS) to Cloudflare Workers. This technique masks the true destination of your traffic, making it appear as though you are accessing a common cloud service rather than a blocked website or application. The project is freely available on GitHub.

item.title: mike
item.description: Mike is an open-source AI platform designed to democratize access to legal information and services. It leverages artificial intelligence to help users understand legal documents, generate legal text, and research legal precedents. The platform aims to provide a free and accessible alternative for individuals and small businesses who might otherwise struggle with the cost and complexity of legal counsel. It offers features such as document summarization, contract analysis, and legal query answering, with a focus on clarity and user-friendliness.

item.title: Ruflo
item.description: Ruflo is an open-source agent orchestration platform from RuvNet built around Claude Code, MCP tooling, and coordinated multi-agent workflows. It adds a plugin marketplace, specialized coding agents, persistent memory, swarm coordination, workflow templates, and federation features that let agents collaborate across projects or machines. The repository positions Ruflo as the successor to Claude Flow, with a CLI, Claude Code plugins, MCP server options, Docker-based self-hosting paths, and hosted demos for chat and goal planning. For developers already using Claude Code or experimenting with autonomous coding assistants, Ruflo acts like a coordination layer: instead of running one assistant at a time, it helps route work through agents for testing, documentation, security review, browser automation, architecture decisions, and background maintenance tasks.

item.title: MasterHttpRelayVPN
item.description: MasterHttpRelayVPN is a sophisticated tool designed to tunnel network traffic through Google Apps Script. It functions as a domain-fronted HTTP/SOCKS5 proxy, enabling users to circumvent network restrictions and censorship by disguising their internet activity. The tool offers advanced features like MITM TLS interception for secure proxying and HTTP/1-2 multiplexing for efficient data transfer. It is particularly effective at evading deep packet inspection (DPI) by making traffic appear as legitimate Google traffic.

item.title: Notion
item.description: Notion is an all-in-one workspace that combines note-taking, project management, and knowledge base functionalities into a single, highly flexible application. Users can create custom databases, documents, and boards to manage tasks, projects, wikis, and more, tailoring the interface to their specific needs. It supports rich media, collaboration features, and a vast array of templates for diverse use cases. Notion offers a free tier for personal use and paid plans for teams and businesses.

item.title: Todoist
item.description: Todoist is a popular, cross-platform task management application designed to help individuals and teams organize their lives and work. It allows users to create to-do lists, set due dates, prioritize tasks, and collaborate with others. The app boasts a clean, intuitive interface and powerful features like natural language input for task creation, recurring dates, labels, and filters. Todoist operates on a freemium model, offering essential functionality for free and advanced features through a paid subscription.

item.title: Visual Diff Tool for Code
item.description: This web-based tool provides a visual comparison of two versions of code, highlighting the differences between them in a clear and intuitive manner. It goes beyond simple text-based diffing by offering side-by-side views with color-coded changes, making it easier to spot and understand modifications. Developers can use this tool to review pull requests, track changes in their projects, or simply compare different code snippets for clarity and accuracy.

item.title: garden-skills
item.description: garden-skills is an open-source collection of skills curated by ConardLi, covering a wide range of topics including web design, knowledge retrieval, image generation, and more. It functions as a repository for valuable insights, techniques, and resources, presented in an accessible format. This project aims to consolidate and share practical knowledge for individuals looking to expand their skillset or find solutions to specific technical challenges. It's a free, community-driven initiative.
